chapter_title.en: What a Person Should Say Upon Waking From Sleep

text: 1387 - كَانَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ إِذَا أَوَى إِلَى فِرَاشِهِ قَالَ « بِاسْمِكَ اَللَّهُمَّ أَحْيَا وَ بِاسْمِكَ أَمُوتُ » فَإِذَا اِسْتَيْقَظَ قَالَ - « اَلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ اَلَّذِي أَحْيَانِي بَعْدَ مَا أَمَاتَنِي «وَ إِلَيْهِ اَلنُّشُورُ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1387 - When the Messenger of Allah (sw) would go to his bed, he would say:
Bismika Allahumma ahya wa bismika amoot
"In Your name, O’ Allah (swt), I live, and in Your name, I die."
And when he would wake up, he would say:
Alhamdulillahi alladhi ahyayani ba‘da ma amatani wa ilayhi an-nushoor
"All praise is due to Allah (swt), who brought me back to life after causing me to die, and to Him is the return."
